name: read-integration-contract-before-changing-config
description: Read source or documentation to understand the integration contract before making configuration changes

name: verify-tool-access-before-delegating-file-tasks
description: Before delegating file-system tasks to subagents, verify required tool access and provide explicit file paths if absent

name: check-document-against-reference-set-with-checklist
description: Check a document against a reference set using a numbered checklist; for each gap, identify the insertion location and draft insertion text

name: verify-null-search-results-with-alternate-method
description: When a search returns no results, run a second search using a different method before concluding no matches exist

name: search-multiple-terms-for-professional-entity-mentions
description: When processing content involving a professional contact, run at least two searches using name variations and related terms before concluding no connected files exist

name: synthesise-new-content-in-context-of-existing-files
description: When adding content from transcripts or notes to a repository, read existing related files first and synthesise what is new

name: update-partial-todo-wording-to-reflect-current-state
description: When sub-items are completed, rewrite the remaining item to reflect only what is left

name: verify-cross-references-after-consolidation
description: After replacing duplicated text with cross-references, verify step/section numbers and unique details are intact

name: include-service-credentials-in-permission-audits
description: When auditing permissions before execution, include service credentials alongside file/tool permissions

name: verify-delegated-work-independently
description: Verify delegated agent output by checking artefacts independently rather than trusting the success report

name: simulate-interface-screen-states
description: Walk through every interaction point rendering progressive screen state, exact text, and UX findings

name: enforce-single-source-constants-in-code
description: Ensure every PRD registry constant has a corresponding entry in constants module; no literal value hardcoded at call sites

name: sync-check-implementation-against-prd-per-step
description: At the end of each implementation step, compare actual signatures, types, errors, and schema against the PRD

name: write-failure-injection-tests-for-multi-step-processes
description: For every multi-step process with a specified partial failure state, write a test that interrupts mid-way and asserts expected recovery

name: identify-exclusion-list-before-public-spec
description: When producing a public version of a technical spec, identify the full exclusion list and present for approval before writing

name: review-documentation-accuracy-before-release
description: Spawn a reviewer against live documentation checking routing, accuracy, completeness, and cross-doc consistency before release

name: assess-scope-before-refining-details
description: Flag when a request describes multiple independent subsystems that need decomposition before detailed design

name: present-design-incrementally-for-approval
description: Present design in sections, seeking approval after each section before proceeding

name: decompose-system-into-single-purpose-units
description: Break a system into units with one clear purpose, defined interfaces, independently testable

name: dispatch-reviewer-with-crafted-context-not-session-history
description: Dispatch review subagent with crafted context, never session history

name: map-file-structure-before-defining-tasks
description: Map which files will be created or modified and their responsibilities before decomposing into tasks

name: run-canon-alignment-as-separate-review-pass
description: Run canon-alignment check as a separate review pass after persona-based review, not merged into it

name: push-back-on-incorrect-review-with-reasoning
description: Push back on incorrect feedback with technical reasoning or evidence

name: forward-compatible-deferral-spec
description: For each deferred decision: what cannot be decided, the current choice, and why it is non-degrading

name: coupling-annotated-feature-list
description: For each coupled pair: feature names, coupling type, one-sentence rationale

name: simulated-interface-walkthrough-with-ux-findings
description: Return a simulated walkthrough showing progressive screen state, typographic layers, exact text, user input with reasoning, and UX findings

name: cross-task-contradiction-report
description: Return report listing each task's key recommendation, contradictions, and recommended resolution

name: schema-verified-prd-section
description: Return PRD section with actual names verified against source, mismatches flagged as blockers, verification evidence table

name: documentation-release-review-report
description: Return review covering routing, accuracy, completeness, and cross-doc consistency

name: incremental-design-with-section-approvals
description: Design document with sections scaled to complexity, each validated before continuing

name: resolve-or-defer-never-ignore
description: When an ambiguity is identified: resolve it with a clear spec or explicitly defer it with a forward-compatible placeholder. Never leave it unaddressed.

name: compare-canon-early-not-late
description: When a project has a canonical reference: compare working documents against it at session start, not at session end

name: preserve-over-overwrite
description: When revising a document: create a new versioned copy rather than overwriting the working draft

name: name-coupling-type-correctly
description: When describing coupling between features: label as architectural (real dependency) or incidental (scheduling convenience), never mislabel

name: author-control-over-speed-in-sequential-review
description: When running sequential review passes: pause for author decisions between rounds rather than running all rounds without stopping

name: one-gate-per-root-cause-not-overlapping
description: When designing process gates after a failure: one gate per root cause at the earliest intervention point, not overlapping gates

name: shared-evaluation-surface-determines-task-boundaries
description: When decomposing tasks for parallel execution: shared evaluation surface means same task; no shared surface means parallel tasks

name: real-use-before-next-prd
description: When finalising the next version PRD: use the current product end-to-end first

name: follow-existing-patterns-over-ideal-structure
description: When modifying a codebase: follow established patterns rather than proposing ideal structure

name: escalate-to-human-after-review-loop-stalls
description: When an automated review loop is not converging: escalate to human rather than continuing to iterate

name: audit-existing-infrastructure-before-composing
description: Inventory available tools, APIs, caches, and data pipelines before designing how an agent will execute a task

name: route-subtasks-to-existing-infrastructure
description: Route subtasks to existing infrastructure (database queries, API endpoints, cached results) instead of composing new agents to replicate that work

name: composition-cost-over-runtime-cost
description: When composition effort and runtime efficiency conflict: invest more in composition design. A cheap-to-compose agent that wastes runtime resources is more expensive than a carefully composed agent that reuses existing infrastructure.

name: precision-tooling-over-brute-force
description: When a specialised tool and a general-purpose agent can both complete a subtask: use the specialised tool. Dispatch a general-purpose agent only when no existing tool covers the requirement.

name: infrastructure-aware-composition-plan
description: Return a composition plan with: task decomposition, infrastructure audit (existing tools/APIs/pipelines per subtask), composition rationale (why each subtask goes to infrastructure or new agent), and expected cost comparison

name: extract-general-before-domain-specific
description: When extracting items from both domain-neutral and domain-specific sources: process domain-neutral sources first to build a base set, then process domain-specific sources comparing against it. General-before-specific prevents duplicate extraction and improves domain-specificity scoring accuracy.

name: dedup-between-extraction-waves
description: When extracting candidates in multiple waves: run a dedup gate between each wave before merging results. Compare each new wave's candidates against all prior waves' accepted candidates at the wave boundary, not at the end.

name: verify-input-counts-before-processing
description: Count items in the received input and reconcile against any stated count in the task header or metadata before operating on the data; flag and correct discrepancies before proceeding

name: version-before-not-after
description: When versioning a document before revision: copy the pre-edit file before making any changes, not after. The pre-edit copy is the version; the working file receives the edits.

name: design-review-criteria-before-composing-reviewer
description: Compose a design agent to identify what a review should check, which cross-references matter, and what the damage profile is before composing the agent that executes the review

name: name-actor-in-operationalisation-claims
description: When specifying that a primitive is operationalised by an endpoint or interface: state whether the endpoint's code consumes the primitive or the requester applies it as reasoning. Do not leave the acting subject implicit.

name: route-enforcement-to-structure-not-content
description: When a desired behaviour requires enforcement: specify the enforcement mechanism in structural configuration (hooks, triage rules, entry-point checks) rather than embedding it in primitive descriptions or agent prompts.

name: absorb-ramble-before-questions
description: Read the user's full unprompted input before asking clarifying questions; only ask about genuine gaps not already covered

name: structure-by-proximity
description: Organise research findings from closest-relevant to most-tangential, with source URLs for each item

name: flag-novelty-vs-existing
description: Explicitly label each finding as novel information or confirmation of what already exists in the user's materials

name: one-sentence-per-outline-point
description: Write each outline item as exactly one sentence plus citation or example references; number every item

name: skeptical-domain-reviewer
description: Adopt the persona of a skeptical domain expert who looks for weaknesses in argument, evidence, and framing

name: three-list-review-format
description: Review output must include three numbered lists: (1) agreements, (2) problems with specific quotes from the draft, (3) fixes tied by number to problems

name: draft-from-outline-and-sources
description: Follow the approved outline point by point, incorporating the user's actual files as source material rather than generating from general knowledge

name: structural-fix-over-content-addition
description: When forced to choose between restructuring existing text and adding new content: the agent should restructure, because content addition requiring domain knowledge or personal experience is the human's job

name: example-grounded-over-abstract-taxonomy
description: When forced to choose between abstract categorical analysis and example-grounded argument: the agent should prefer example-grounded argument, using specific cases from the user's materials rather than generating taxonomic prose

name: human-only-content-flagged
description: Output must flag every point where personal detail, domain-specific framing, or example-grounded argument is needed, marking these as requiring human input rather than attempting generation

name: parallel-independent-execution
description: Run each subtask via two independent methods and merge results into a single output

name: agreement-as-validation
description: When forced to choose between trusting a single method's output and requiring cross-method agreement: the agent should require agreement before treating a finding as validated

name: voice-vs-argument-architecture
description: When forced to choose between preserving the author's voice and strengthening argument architecture: the agent should fix architecture, because voice is easier for the human to restore than structure is to rebuild

name: calibrate-judge-scale-explicitly
description: Include an explicit calibration note in judge prompts specifying what each score level means and requiring use of the full scale including 9 and 10

name: plateau-detection-signal
description: Evaluation output must note when scores have not improved by more than 0.5 across two consecutive rounds, signalling that the current approach has hit a ceiling

name: meta-prompt-diagnosis
description: Diagnose each evaluation criterion's specific gap relative to the current artifact and reference materials

name: multi-criteria-simultaneous-edit
description: Produce targeted edits addressing all diagnosed gaps in a single pass rather than serially

name: meta-prompt-targets-all-criteria
description: The designed prompt must address every evaluation criterion in a single pass, not serially, to avoid the fix-one-break-another pattern

name: terminology-discipline-pass
description: Scan the full document for every instance of each introduced term and enforce consistent usage throughout

name: terminology-variance-report
description: Output must list each term, its canonical form, and every variant found with line locations

name: compression-vs-information-retention
description: When forced to choose between shorter text and retaining specific examples and evidence: the agent should retain the examples, cutting abstract connective prose instead

name: human-edit-vs-agent-generation-at-ceiling
description: When forced to choose between continuing agent iteration on a plateaued artifact and returning it to the human with specific gap annotations: the agent should return to the human, because remaining gaps require personal detail, domain-specific framing, or subjective judgement that further agent rounds will not resolve

name: diagnose-before-fixing
description: Before producing edits, output a diagnosis stating what specifically is wrong with each evaluation criterion and why previous fixes did not resolve it

name: propose-hypothesis-before-mechanism
description: Propose a falsifiable hypothesis statement before designing the scoring or evaluation mechanism that implements it

name: pilot-on-small-sample-before-full-run
description: Apply a new methodology to a random 5-15% sample before running on the full dataset

name: re-pilot-on-fresh-sample-after-revision
description: After revising a methodology based on pilot results, re-pilot on a fresh sample that excludes previously scored items

name: measure-variance-to-evaluate-instrument-quality
description: Compute variance and score distribution after each pilot to determine whether the scoring instrument discriminates adequately among candidates

name: design-screening-gate-to-reduce-pool
description: Design a pass/fail screening instrument to reduce the candidate pool before fine-grained ranking

name: design-selection-ranker-for-screened-pool
description: Design a separate ranking instrument to discriminate within the pool that passed the screening gate

name: cluster-candidates-by-semantic-overlap-before-selecting
description: Group candidates by semantic similarity and identify redundancy clusters before selecting a final set

name: enforce-subagent-compliance-via-explicit-constraints
description: When a subagent fails to comply with a scoring rule, add an explicit constraint to the prompt and re-run rather than accepting non-compliant output

name: document-methodology-as-work-progresses
description: Write methodology documentation incrementally as each stage completes, not as a retrospective summary after all work is done

name: instrument-discrimination-report
description: Return a discrimination report with: per-dimension variance, score distribution histogram, number of distinct score values, and comparison to prior instrument version if applicable

name: ranked-shortlist-with-cluster-deduplication
description: Return a ranked shortlist where each semantic cluster is represented by exactly one member, with cluster membership and the representative selection rationale documented alongside the ranking

name: gate-then-rank-over-single-instrument
description: When a single scoring instrument saturates at the top of its range: split into a screening gate (pass/fail) and a selection ranker (fine discrimination among passers) rather than redesigning the original instrument to do both

name: small-pilot-before-full-run
description: When applying a new or revised scoring methodology to a large dataset: run a 5-15% pilot first and review variance before committing to the full run. A wasted pilot is cheaper than a full run with a broken instrument.

name: variance-over-accuracy-when-calibrating-instruments
description: When calibrating a scoring instrument: prioritise variance over accuracy. An instrument that assigns the same score to different candidates cannot discriminate regardless of how accurate each individual score is.

name: cluster-representative-over-top-n-individual
description: When selecting a final set from ranked candidates that contain semantic clusters: select one representative per cluster rather than taking the top-N by individual score. A set of near-duplicates at the top of the ranking provides less coverage than a diverse set drawn from multiple clusters.

name: type-quota-as-hard-constraint-on-set-composition
description: When composing a set from candidates that belong to multiple categories: enforce a minimum quota per category as a hard constraint before optimising for individual score. An unbalanced set that excels in one category but neglects others fails the set-level objective even if individual scores are high.

name: accept-instrument-ceiling-when-screening-purpose-is-met
description: When a screening instrument correctly separates pass from fail but clusters passers at the top of the range: accept the ceiling rather than redesigning the instrument. Design a second-stage selection instrument instead.

name: classify-task-before-selecting
description: Classify the task by type before selecting primitives; use the classification to weight selection

name: verify-composition-fitness
description: After selecting primitives, verify at least one role component directly relates to the task's core action

name: boost-by-skill-tags
description: Use skill tags from the task assignment as a boost signal during primitive selection

name: relevance-floor-over-slot-filling
description: When the best-matching primitive for a composition slot has similarity below 0.5 to the task description: leave the slot empty rather than filling it with a low-relevance primitive. Fewer relevant primitives outperform more irrelevant ones

name: composition-with-fitness-metadata
description: Return the composition with fitness metadata: per-primitive similarity scores, task-type classification, and a pool_coverage_warning flag set to true if no primitive in any slot exceeded similarity 0.6

name: justify-bypass-not-composition
description: When a task feels too simple or mechanical to warrant composition: invert the burden of proof. The default is to compose. To skip, name the specific primitives the assigner would have selected and explain why none improve the outcome. If you cannot name them, compose.

name: enumerate-applicable-primitives-before-bypass
description: Before deciding a task does not need composition, list the primitives semantic similarity would match to the task description; if any matched primitive addresses a failure mode the direct approach does not, composition is required

name: instruction-not-documentation
description: An agent doing this task will include only the actionable directive, never rationale, formulas, or procedural elaboration.

name: fix-at-source-not-symptom
description: When a failure has a known root cause: fix at the source; do not address symptoms while the cause remains

name: identify-unresolved-decisions
description: Distinguish between decisions that are genuinely resolved and those presented as resolved but still open

name: ask-purpose-and-failure-before-fixing
description: Ask what the thing is for and what breaks if the fix is wrong before proposing a resolution to any ambiguity or gap

name: design-review-criteria-before-composing-reviewer
description: Compose a design agent to identify what a review should check, which cross-references matter, and what the damage profile is before composing the agent that executes the review

name: forward-compatible-deferral-spec
description: For each deferred decision: what cannot be decided, the current choice, and why it is non-degrading

name: infrastructure-aware-composition-plan
description: Return a composition plan with: task decomposition, infrastructure audit (existing tools/APIs/pipelines per subtask), composition rationale (why each subtask goes to infrastructure or new agent), and expected cost comparison

name: iteration-history-with-deltas
description: Return an iteration history containing: for each iteration, the iteration number, composition used (with diff from previous), per-dimension scores, aggregate score, delta from previous iteration, and the primitive(s) added or changed. End with a convergence summary: total improvement from baseline, iterations to reach current state, and whether the improvement trend is increasing, stable, or diminishing.

name: pairing-level-over-component-level
description: When evaluating resource investments where value emerges from combinations: assess at the pairing or portfolio level, not at the individual component level. Component-level optimisation produces systematically suboptimal composites when value is combinatorial.

name: compose-before-dispatching
description: When forced to choose between dispatching a subagent directly and composing it through Agency first: always compose through Agency. Agency-composed agents measurably outperform uncomposed ones (55/60 vs 26/60 on 12-dimension evaluation). The composition step adds minutes; the quality gain is categorical, not marginal.

name: flag-misplaced-content-for-relocation
description: Treat content found in the wrong location as a rehoming problem, not a deletion problem; flag it for relocation rather than removing it

name: translate-gap-into-candidate-primitive
description: For each attributed performance gap, draft a candidate primitive that would close it: select the type (role_component / desired_outcome / trade_off_config), write the description using the structural test, and specify which rubric dimension it targets

name: detect-dropped-content-across-versions
description: After a document revision, identify content present in the previous version that is absent from the new version

name: generate-primitive-only-for-persistent-gaps
description: When a gap appears in a single comparison run: note it but do not generate a primitive. Generate primitives only for gaps that persist across two or more comparison runs on different task instances. Premature primitive generation creates noise in the primitive set.

name: verify-input-counts-before-processing
description: Count items in the received input and reconcile against any stated count in the task header or metadata before operating on the data; flag and correct discrepancies before proceeding

name: scrutinise-strong-consensus
description: When agreement is unusually strong or fast: increase scrutiny rather than accepting it; treat strong consensus as a signal to probe harder

name: test-one-variable-at-a-time
description: Make the smallest possible change to test each hypothesis; change one variable at a time

name: provide-context-not-hedges
description: When tempted to hedge a claim: provide specific conditions or caveats instead; vague hedges obscure rather than calibrate precision

name: isolate-generator-context
description: Give the generator only the current candidate and the actionable single improvement from the last judge; never pass score history

name: isolate-judge-context
description: Give the judge only the current candidate, criteria, and seed calibration; never pass intermediate candidates

name: no-anchoring-in-iterative-evaluation
description: The judge's scores must reflect the candidate against criteria and seed calibration, never against prior iteration scores

name: score-via-seed-calibration
description: Score the original draft first as seed calibration, then score all subsequent iterations against criteria definitions anchored to that seed

name: run-side-by-side-on-same-input
description: When comparing two agent compositions, run both on identical input with identical feedback, then have a single judge score both outputs in the same pass

name: merge-best-passages-from-candidates
description: The final output must incorporate the highest-scoring passage for each evaluation dimension rather than selecting one candidate whole

name: coherence-check-after-merge
description: After merging passages from different candidates, verify there are no seams, contradictions, duplicated claims, orphaned references, or tonal shifts at merge points

name: full-rewrite-then-surgical
description: When forced to choose between full rewrite and surgical edit: use full rewrites for the first 2-3 iterations, then switch to surgical edits once scores stabilise

name: protect-high-scoring-passages
description: Name passages scoring 9+ in the generator prompt and constrain edits within them to words or sentences containing a specific identified violation

name: structural-over-sentence-after-plateau
description: When forced to choose between continuing sentence-level fixes and switching to structural edits after 2-3 rounds with no score movement: switch to structural edits

name: meta-prompt-to-break-plateau
description: When 2+ iterations produce no score movement, spawn an agent to redesign the generator prompt rather than re-running the same prompt structure

name: sequential-multi-pass-architecture
description: Structure the generator as one pass per criterion, each pass operating on the previous pass's output, rather than addressing all criteria in a single pass

name: no-positioning-disclaiming-announcing-hammering
description: The output must not contain sentences that position, disclaim, announce, or hammer. Each flagged sentence must be tested by removal: if the paragraph reads stronger without it, the sentence stays removed

name: author-handles-credibility-boundaries
description: When forced to choose between generator autonomy and author intervention on claims of defensibility, qualifier placement, and register crossing from sharing into selling: defer to the author

name: document-composition-differences-as-hypotheses
description: Identify which primitives differ between two compositions and document each difference as a testable hypothesis about its effect on output quality

name: labelled-comparison-outputs
description: Each output in a comparison run must include agent identifier, timestamp, task ID, and the composition used

name: step-boundary-by-output-format-change
description: Place a step boundary where the output format changes (e.g. text to image, metadata to prose). Combining format-heterogeneous operations into one step produces tangled error handling and makes the step unreviewable by a single method.

name: step-boundary-by-human-decision
description: Place a step boundary wherever a human decision is needed before proceeding. Bundling automatic work after a decision point with automatic work before it forces the human to either approve everything or restart from scratch.

name: step-boundary-by-irreversibility
description: Place a step boundary before any irreversible operation (writing to external system, publishing, sending). Everything before the boundary can be re-run; everything after cannot. This is the last safe review point.

name: step-boundary-by-input-source
description: Place a step boundary where inputs switch sources. Steps with single-source inputs are independently testable.

name: combine-same-input-no-decision-steps
description: Combine adjacent steps that share the same input, require no human decision, and produce intermediate results consumed only by the next step. Splitting them creates unnecessary review points and slows execution without adding safety.

name: human-gate-on-subjective-choice
description: Require human approval when multiple valid options exist and the choice is subjective. Automating subjective choices produces technically valid but personally wrong results.

name: human-gate-on-external-write
description: Require human approval before any write to a system the user must own. The agent assembles; the human authorises the push.

name: human-gate-on-lossy-transformation
description: Require human approval before lossy transformations (PDF to PNG, audio to transcript). The agent shows before/after; the human confirms acceptable fidelity.

name: human-gate-on-public-output
description: Require human approval on any output visible to the public. Even if all components were individually approved, the assembled whole may read differently.

name: automatic-when-deterministic-reversible-internal
description: When a step is deterministic, reversible, and internal: proceed without a human gate. Adding unnecessary gates trains the user to rubber-stamp, which degrades the signal value of gates that matter.

name: gate-format-ranked-list-for-selection
description: Present selection tasks as numbered lists ordered best-first. This format lets the user reply with just a number.

name: gate-format-before-after-for-transformation
description: Present transformation approvals as before/after pairs so the user can see exactly what changed.

name: gate-format-full-block-for-approval
description: Present approval tasks as the full text block the user will be committing to. Summaries of what will be written hide details the user would catch in the actual text.

name: manual-execution-before-codification
description: When codifying a process as a skill or automation: execute it manually on a real case first. The manual run surfaces requirements that conversational description misses because habitual steps are invisible to the practitioner.

name: targeted-clarifying-questions-after-execution
description: After the manual run, ask clarifying questions targeting edge cases that execution surfaced. These questions are sharper than pre-execution questions because they reference concrete situations.

name: codify-corrections-not-initial-description
description: The codified skill must encode the corrected process (initial description + all real-time corrections + clarification answers), not the initial description alone.

name: encode-automatic-vs-gated-per-step
description: Each step in the codified skill must explicitly declare whether it is automatic or human-gated. Leaving this implicit means different executions of the same skill may gate differently.

name: process-describer
description: Elicit the user's description of their process without interrupting or restructuring. The initial description is the user's mental model; its structure reveals which steps the user considers important and which they consider background.

name: manual-executor
description: Execute the described process step by step on a real case, flagging each point where the description is ambiguous or incomplete. Do not invent answers; pause and ask.

name: correction-integrator
description: After manual execution, merge the initial description with all corrections and clarification answers into a single consistent process. Detect contradictions between corrections and flag them.

name: skill-encoder
description: Write the codified skill with all structural elements specified by other primitives in the composition, plus common-mistakes documentation derived from errors made during the manual run.

name: common-mistakes-from-manual-run
description: Document common mistakes in the skill based on actual errors made during the manual execution, not hypothetical ones.

name: description-first-vs-execution-first
description: Elicit a conversational description before manual execution rather than jumping straight to execution. The description provides a scaffold that makes corrections more efficient.

name: skill-evolution-on-process-change
description: When the underlying process changes after codification, update the skill rather than working around it. Stale skills train agents to ignore skill instructions, degrading compliance across all skills.

name: verify-on-second-case-before-deployment
description: After codifying a process from one manual run, execute the skill on a second real case before considering it validated. Single-case codification overfits to the first case's specific conditions.

name: common-mistakes-when-manual-run-produced-errors
description: Include a common-mistakes section in the codified skill only when the manual execution produced actual errors. Hypothetical mistakes sections add length without adding signal.

name: strip-internal-from-public-docs
description: Strip internal implementation details (database schemas, algorithm thresholds, encoding formats, internal agent mechanics) from public-facing documents, retaining only what requesters need: API schemas, CLI commands, configuration options, and migration steps.

name: enforce-terminology-consistency
description: Enforce a single canonical term for each concept across all documents in a deliverable set; identify and replace synonyms, noting the canonical term and its definition.

name: verify-no-unintended-agent-edits
description: After any agent completes, check all modified files against the agent's explicit write scope; flag and revert modifications to files the agent was only instructed to read.

name: check-artifacts-for-pii-before-release
description: Scan all bundled artifacts (skill files, config templates, example files) for PII, private filesystem paths, internal repo references, and user-specific values before public release.

name: minimal-public-surface-over-full-disclosure
description: When forced to choose between exposing internal architecture and providing sufficient user documentation: document only what requesters need to integrate (endpoints, schemas, CLI flags, migration steps), not how the system works internally.

name: logical-grouping-over-implementation-sequence
description: When forced to choose between organising release documentation by implementation sequence (waves, sprints, phases) and by user-facing function (bug fixes, features, setup, migration): prefer logical function grouping.

name: narrate-each-step-before-executing
description: Before executing each step of a multistep process, state what you are about to do and why, so the user can follow the sequence without reading the implementation

name: show-intermediate-output-not-just-final
description: After each stage of a multistep process, display the intermediate output before proceeding to the next stage

name: distinguish-system-status-from-user-action
description: Visually or structurally separate system progress messages from points where the user must act or decide

name: frame-each-phase-with-purpose-before-detail
description: Before presenting the steps within a phase, state what the phase accomplishes and why it matters; detail follows purpose

name: test-on-fresh-install-not-dev-environment
description: After implementing a fix or feature, verify it works by installing from the distribution channel into a clean environment, not by running from the development tree

name: trace-data-through-all-layers-not-just-endpoints
description: When verifying a fix, trace the data through every layer of the stack, not just the layer where the fix was applied

name: test-with-realistic-data-volume-not-empty-state
description: Run integration tests against a database with realistic data volume and history, not just freshly seeded minimal state

name: reproduce-in-user-environment-before-declaring-fixed
description: Before declaring a bug fixed, reproduce the original failure in an environment matching the user's setup, not just in the test suite

name: check-threshold-against-actual-data-distribution
description: When a system uses a numeric threshold, verify it against the actual distribution of values in production data, not assumed or theoretical ranges

name: separate-pre-and-post-implementation-issues
description: Maintain separate issue files for pre-implementation requirements and post-implementation defects; never merge them into a single file

name: update-issue-status-at-time-of-fix
description: When fixing an issue, update its status in the issue file immediately — in the same work unit as the fix, not in a later cleanup pass

name: categorise-deferred-items-with-rationale-and-destination
description: When deferring an issue to a future version, record what is deferred, why, and which specific future milestone it is deferred to

name: distinguish-unimplemented-from-broken
description: When an expected feature is absent, investigate whether it was never implemented or was implemented and broke; record the finding because the fix paths differ

name: assess-audience-knowledge-level
description: Before explaining, identify what the audience already knows and what they do not; begin the explanation at the boundary between known and unknown

name: use-analogy-from-audience-domain
description: Explain unfamiliar concepts using analogies drawn from the audience's own domain or daily experience rather than from the expert's domain

name: anticipate-common-misconceptions
description: Identify the most likely misconception the audience will form and address it explicitly before it takes root

name: explain-causal-mechanism-not-just-outcome
description: Explain WHY something happens (the mechanism) not just WHAT happens (the outcome); trace the causal chain step by step

name: use-contrast-to-sharpen-boundaries
description: Clarify what something is by explicitly contrasting it with what it is not or what it is easily confused with

name: provide-a-worked-example
description: Walk through one complete concrete example from start to finish before stating general rules or principles

name: name-what-changes-and-what-stays-fixed
description: When explaining a process or system, explicitly identify which elements vary and which remain constant across instances

name: connect-to-audience-motivation
description: State why the audience should care about this explanation before delivering it; link the concept to a problem they face or a question they have

name: signal-importance-levels
description: Explicitly mark which parts of an explanation are essential, which are helpful context, and which are optional detail

name: close-with-a-comprehension-check
description: End an explanation with a question or scenario that tests whether the audience grasped the core concept

name: walkthrough-with-pause-points
description: Return a step-by-step walkthrough where each step contains: what happens, what the user sees, and a pause point awaiting user confirmation before proceeding

name: fix-verification-with-layer-trace
description: Return a fix verification report with: bug description, fix applied, layer fixed, trace showing data passes correctly through every layer, and the test that would have caught the bug originally

name: issue-file-with-status-and-fix-trace
description: Return an issue tracking file where each issue contains: title, status, files changed, root cause, fix description or deferral rationale, and commit hash

name: explanation-with-analogy-and-counterexample
description: Return an explanation containing: core concept in plain language, one analogy from everyday experience, one concrete example, and one counterexample showing what the concept excludes

name: causal-chain-explanation
description: Return a causal explanation as a numbered chain where each step states what happens, why it happens, and what it causes next, ending with the observable outcome

name: misconception-correction-output
description: Return a misconception correction containing: the wrong belief stated clearly, why it seems plausible, the specific point where it breaks down, and the correct understanding with evidence

name: clarity-of-process-over-speed-of-completion
description: When a multistep process can be completed faster by skipping explanations: keep the explanations. A user who understands what happened can troubleshoot and teach others; a user who got a fast result cannot

name: expected-state-then-verify-then-act
description: When giving instructions to execute: state the expected outcome first, then the verification command, then the action command. Never give an action without a way to verify the result

name: end-to-end-test-over-layer-test
description: When choosing between a test that validates one layer in isolation and a test that exercises the full path from user input to user output: write the end-to-end test first

name: track-per-issue-not-per-batch
description: When multiple issues are found in a single testing session: record each issue separately with its own status and root cause, not as a batch summary

name: file-per-milestone-not-single-growing-file
description: When tracking issues across milestones: create a separate file per milestone rather than appending to a single growing file

name: accuracy-over-simplicity-at-decision-points
description: When simplification would change what the audience decides or does: preserve accuracy even at the cost of a harder explanation

name: familiar-analogy-over-precise-analogy
description: When choosing between a technically precise but unfamiliar analogy and a slightly imprecise but immediately graspable one: use the graspable one and note where it breaks down

name: one-good-example-over-many-partial
description: When choosing between one fully worked example and several partially worked examples: complete the single example

name: address-misconception-before-advancing
description: When the audience holds a misconception that will interfere with understanding the next concept: correct it before introducing new material, even if it interrupts the planned sequence

name: check-commit-origin-before-discarding
description: Before discarding file changes with git checkout, restore, or reset, check git log for the file to determine whether the most recent commit is from the current working context; display the commit and ask before discarding if it is not.

name: verify-working-directory-on-empty-search
description: When a search returns zero results for a target expected to exist, verify the working directory matches the intended repository before concluding the target is absent.

name: investigate-before-discard
description: When forced to choose between discarding unexpected file changes immediately and investigating their origin first: always investigate. The cost of checking git log is seconds; the cost of discarding a legitimate change from another source is re-implementation and possible re-tagging.

name: verify-quantitative-claims-against-primary-sources
description: Verify numerical claims (prices, rates, measurements, specs) against authoritative primary sources before treating them as reliable

name: isolate-structural-constraints-into-dedicated-agent
description: When a task has asymmetric or compound constraints (different rules for different directions, different stakeholders for different phases), isolate that complexity into a dedicated agent rather than embedding it in a general analysis

name: quantitative-verification-report
description: Return a verification report for numerical claims with: claim, initial value, primary source consulted, primary source value, match status (exact/discrepant/unverifiable), and corrected value where applicable

name: verify-quantitative-before-relying
description: When forced to choose between trusting agent-researched numerical data and running a verification pass against primary sources: always verify. Initial quantitative research can be significantly off; the verification cost is low relative to the cost of acting on wrong numbers

name: compose-regardless-of-task-domain
description: When a task is non-software (research, analysis, planning) and composition feels unnecessary: compose through Agency anyway. Task domain is never a valid reason to skip composition; the composition step is lightweight and the quality gain applies to all task types

name: verify-when-primary-source-exists
description: When a claim can be checked against a primary source (API, official site, database): always check it. Claims that matched primary sources were exact; claims not checkable against them were the ones that turned out wrong

name: dedicated-agent-over-general-for-compound-constraints
description: When a task has asymmetric or compound structural constraints: assign a dedicated agent to the constrained subtask rather than embedding the complexity in a general agent. General agents systematically underweight structural complexity

name: audience-contextualized-evaluation
description: When evaluating writing or documentation, adopt the specific mental model, technical level, and arrival context of the stated target reader. Score against what that reader needs, not against abstract quality criteria.

name: evidence-cascade-for-examples
description: When a task requires concrete examples or proof points, follow this preference cascade: (1) search the project's own history first — review findings, evaluation scores, early drafts, client memos, scope documents, bug catches, session logs; (2) search for real-world examples with validated sourcing; (3) construct hypotheticals based on user-provided or fetched validated data. Never fabricate unsourced examples.

name: scoring-precision-calibration
description: When reporting composite scores from multi-criterion evaluation, use at least one more decimal place than the per-criterion increment to avoid masking real movement between iterations.

name: concept-first-vs-problem-first-ordering
description: When the target reader lacks the mental model to recognise the problem: lead with a brief category-setter before the problem statement. When the reader already has the framework: lead with the problem. The test is whether the reader has the vocabulary to feel the pain without context.

name: simultaneous-multi-criteria-over-serial-asi
description: When iterative single-fix rounds plateau (score movement less than 0.5 across two consecutive rounds): switch to a meta-prompt that diagnoses all criteria gaps simultaneously and produces one set of coordinated edits. Serial fix-one-break-another is the expected failure mode of single-ASI iteration.

name: separation-prevents-confirmation-bias
description: When benchmarking practice against a reference, the observer must not know the benchmark while observing, and the evaluator must see both without having produced either. Trades integration convenience for independence of judgement.

name: pre-digestion-over-autonomous-comprehension
description: Summarise relevant source material with key findings and location references in the agent prompt rather than expecting the agent to parse independently. Trades prompt preparation effort for higher-quality analysis output — the agent spends capacity on analysis not comprehension.

name: match-gap-identification-separation
description: Split what matches from what has no counterpart into distinct evaluation steps. Combining both in one pass biases toward confirming matches and underreports genuine gaps. Trades evaluation speed for completeness of gap detection.

name: clean-gap-list-as-downstream-input
description: The gap identification step should produce a list usable as direct input for verification or further research, not a narrative requiring further parsing.

name: independence-of-observation-and-evaluation
description: Each pipeline stage produces output without access to prior stages conclusions, ensuring that cross-evaluation reflects genuine convergence rather than anchoring.

name: sequential-pipeline-analyst
description: Structures analytical work as a multi-stage pipeline where each agents output feeds the next, with role separation enforcing independence between extraction observation cross-evaluation and verification stages.

name: source-pre-digester
description: Reads and summarises long documents into structured findings with location references, producing condensed input for downstream analysis agents.

name: agency-composition-for-analysis-not-just-implementation
description: Apply Agency primitive selection to analytical research and evaluation tasks not only code implementation. Trades the assumption that composition overhead is only worthwhile for build tasks for broader coverage of task types where structured agent framing improves output.

name: find-all-dependents-before-changing-referenced-value
description: When modifying any value that other artifacts reference (string literals, config values, API contracts, cross-references, shared constants): find all dependents before committing to the change, not after downstream breakage surfaces. A targeted dependency search is always cheaper than discovering breakage in production or review.

name: verify-indirection-resolves-to-current-state
description: Before acting on any symbolic reference (tag, alias, URL, cached value, pointer, branch ref), verify it resolves to the intended current state. Indirection goes stale silently.

name: prefer-freshness-over-speed-at-delivery-boundaries
description: When freshness and speed conflict at a publish, release, or handoff moment: prefer freshness. The cost of updating a pointer is trivial; the cost of delivering stale content is a correction cycle.

name: exhaust-project-local-records-before-searching-broadly
description: Check the projects own conventions files, continuity records, configuration, and commit history before searching broadly or asking the requester for information that may already be recorded.

name: update-continuity-records-inline-not-batched
description: When maintaining project records and progressing on work conflict: update records as milestones happen, not batched at session end. Deferred updates lose detail, and a session that ends unexpectedly loses all of it.

name: assess-dependency-footprint-before-proposing-change
description: Before proposing a fix or modification, identify what depends on the thing being changed and surface the impact to the requester. The question is not can I change this but what else moves if I do.

name: encode-search-order-from-corrections-not-just-immediate-fix
description: When a requester corrects an information-seeking approach: the correction encodes a preferred search order that applies to all future similar queries, not just the immediate instance. Record the ordering rule, not just the answer found.

name: develop-execute-verification-strategy-per-claim
description: For each extracted claim, develop a verification strategy specifying what to search for and what evidence would confirm or disconfirm, execute the strategy, and assess support level against authoritative sources

name: compress-citations-to-collective-footnote
description: Compress multiple citations making the same collective point into a single body-text sentence stating what they collectively demonstrate, with a footnote holding individual references, links, and distinguishing details

name: structured-copy-edit-report
description: Return a structured report with three bounded sections: Keep (up to N items, each citing specific text and explaining why it works), Change (up to N items, each citing text, naming the problem, and proposing a concrete fix), Cut (up to N items, each citing text and explaining why removal improves the piece)

name: depth-for-advancing-citations-compression-for-rest
description: When compressing a literature review with 5+ citations: retain the 2-3 citations that most directly advance the argument at full depth in body text; fold the rest into a collective reference with a footnote holding details and links

name: pause-for-author-on-subjective-editorial-choices
description: When a proposed editorial change could be resolved by the agent or requires a subjective authorial choice: present it as a decision question with 2-3 options and trade-offs rather than choosing autonomously, because wasted prose work on a wrong subjective assumption costs more than the delay of asking

name: adopt-client-persona-for-review
description: Evaluate a deliverable from a specific named client's perspective, grounded in their political context, organisational position, reading habits, and decision-making concerns — not a generic stakeholder review

name: annotate-tease-vs-resolve-per-section
description: Before drafting a consulting deliverable, mark each section as resolve fully (give the complete answer) or tease (name the problem, do not prescribe the solution) based on whether the section delivers value or opens a thread for continued engagement

name: decompose-writing-by-skill-type
description: Break a multi-section writing task into subtasks grouped by the kind of writing required (diagnostic analysis, factual reportage, framework exposition, operational tables, action recommendations) rather than by section number or document order

name: structured-keep-change-cut-missing-review
description: Return a review with exactly four sections of equal length: N things to keep (with rationale), N things to change (with proposed rewrites), N things to cut (with reasoning), N things missing (grounded in broader context beyond the document under review)

name: resolve-operational-tease-strategic
description: When a consulting deliverable must both deliver value and create demand for further work: fully resolve operational outputs (gap tables, action items, factual records, process sequences) and tease strategic threads (frameworks, capability gaps, structural tensions). If uncertain whether something is operational or strategic, resolve it — withholding operational content feels dishonest

name: genuine-value-over-visible-pitch
description: When inserting business development threads into a consulting deliverable: each addition must deliver genuine analytical value that the client would want even if they never hired the consultant again. If the addition only makes sense as a setup for further engagement, cut it — the client noticing a pitch is worse than missing a BD opportunity

name: frame-abstract-exercises-with-concrete-stopping-conditions
description: When designing a process, exercise, or analytical activity that risks feeling academic or abstract to participants, open with concrete stopping conditions that connect the exercise to the participants' actual operational problem

name: parallel-revision-tracks-then-single-integration
description: When a document requires multiple types of revision (client-requested changes, cuts, additions): run revision tracks in parallel as separate agents, then integrate all changes in a single assembly pass. Do not apply changes sequentially — sequential application causes each track to work against a moving target

name: writing-plan-before-writing-agents
description: Before dispatching writing agents for a multi-section document, produce an enriched writing plan that specifies for each section: what it must say, its structural role, specific evidence to cite, tone guidance, connections to other sections, and length target — detailed enough that writing agents can execute without asking questions

name: task-decomposition-agent-before-parallel-dispatch
description: Before dispatching parallel agents for a complex task, spawn a decomposition agent that reads the full brief and groups work into subtasks by skill type required, producing self-contained task descriptions for each — do not decompose manually

name: client-feedback-drives-revision-not-author-judgement
description: When the client provides feedback on a deliverable: implement the feedback as given. The client's political context is information the author lacks. When client feedback and author instinct conflict: prefer client feedback

name: enforce-full-scoring-range
description: When scoring outputs on a numeric scale, use the full range — anchor to behavioural level descriptions, maintain gaps proportional to actual quality differences, and avoid compressing scores into the upper quartile

name: validate-extracted-metrics
description: After extracting numeric values from structured text, validate that all values are present, non-negative, within plausible ranges, and of expected type before using them in computation; log raw source text for any value that fails validation

name: comparative-evaluation-validity-report
description: Return an evaluation validity report with: for each scoring dimension, whether it measures the stated research question (YES/NO with evidence); score variance risk assessment; weight sensitivity analysis under 3+ schemes; confound inventory with controlled/uncontrolled status; sample size adequacy for stated claims

name: composite-for-classification-pairwise-for-preference
description: When a multi-dimensional composite score and a holistic preference judgement disagree on which candidate won: use the composite for formal classification (it accounts for cost dimensions) and the preference for ranking (it reflects what the evaluator would actually choose). Report both; do not force agreement.

name: prompt-enforcement-with-compliance-check
description: When a behaviour restriction cannot be enforced programmatically on a spawned agent or subprocess: use explicit instructions to prohibit the behaviour, plus post-hoc verification from logs or output metadata. Accept soft enforcement as sufficient when hard enforcement is unavailable; flag violations for re-run rather than treating them as failures.

name: composition-execution-separation
description: Separate agent composition (agency_assign) from agent execution; use the rendered prompt's role framing, trade-off awareness, and task structure to guide inline execution when subagent dispatch is blocked by path restrictions, permission rules, or single-file constraints

name: inline-over-uncomposed-when-dispatch-blocked
description: When subagent dispatch is blocked (path restrictions, permission denial, stay-in-repo mandate): execute the task inline using the Agency-composed rendered prompt as operating instructions, rather than executing without composition. Composition overhead is low (one agency_assign call); loss of role framing and trade-off awareness is high.

name: verify-numerical-claims-against-sources
description: Trace each numerical claim in a research document back to its original source, checking paper existence, number accuracy, and source attribution correctness; flag misattributions where a claim is attributed to the wrong source

name: adapt-research-synthesis-for-non-domain-audience
description: Before producing a write-up of evaluation results, identify the target audience (technical, investor, funder, internal, academic) and adapt: level of detail, terminology, which metrics to foreground, and what context to include vs assume known

name: assess-ecosystem-parameters-for-capital-intensive-industry
description: When evaluating an industry ecosystem's readiness for self-sustainability, identify the industry's capital intensity (development cycle length, per-unit cost, failure rate, regulatory burden) and adjust all general ecosystem thresholds upward proportionally

name: propose-research-direction-not-individual-fetches
description: When operating in ask-first web research mode, propose the research direction and scope at the start, then execute freely within the approved direction rather than requesting permission for each individual fetch

name: treat-seeded-parameters-as-launch-points
description: When given initial parameters (interests, topics, constraints) as starting configuration, treat them as launch points for exploration rather than boundaries constraining it

name: fall-back-to-direct-execution-on-subagent-failure
description: When a dispatched subagent fails (rate limit, timeout, crash) before producing output, check for partial results in the output file, then execute the work directly in the main session rather than re-spawning

name: reframe-insufficient-target-as-milestone
description: When a client's quantitative target is empirically too small for the stated goal, frame it as a credible milestone on the way rather than an incorrect target, preserving the client's current work while redirecting their planning horizon

name: embed-verified-live-links-inline
description: Embed hyperlinks to verified live sources directly in prose where claims are made, verifying URL liveness before embedding; prefer inline linking over footnotes or bibliographies for research-backed deliverables

name: audience-adapted-research-memo
description: Return a research memo with: framing question (one sentence), evidence synthesis (structured by argument not by source), specific numbers with inline source links, implications for the reader's context, and internal links to preparatory documents

name: milestone-framing-over-correction-in-client-deliverables
description: When forced to choose between directly correcting a client's target and reframing it as a stage in a longer journey: prefer the reframe, as it preserves the client relationship and produces the same planning outcome without political cost

name: direction-autonomy-over-per-action-permission
description: When forced to choose between granular per-action permission requests and direction-level permission with autonomous execution within scope: prefer direction-level permission, as per-action requests interrupt flow and signal lack of trust without adding safety

name: select-structurally-precise-element-from-source
description: When extracting a summary element from a longer passage, select the sentence that names the structural cause or mechanism rather than mechanically extracting the opening or most prominent clause

name: identify-cross-cutting-theme-across-discrete-findings
description: When producing a list of discrete findings, issues, or gaps, name the structural theme that connects them before or alongside the individual items

name: design-proactive-detection-for-power-asymmetric-relationships
description: When designing safeguards, feedback mechanisms, or review processes where one party has less power than the other, propose mechanisms that go to the less-powerful party rather than requiring them to initiate contact

name: verify-constraints-against-implementation-patterns
description: When reviewing a specification that defines both constraints (schemas, validation rules, type systems, allowlists) and implementation patterns (code examples, API calls, data flows), check that values produced by the implementation patterns satisfy the constraints

name: return-interventions-classified-by-obligation-type
description: Return proposed interventions with each classified by obligation type (legally required, contractually required, reputationally defensive, operationally prudent), specifying the lightest-weight mechanism that creates the needed visibility or control

name: thoroughness-over-speed-in-review
description: When specification thoroughness conflicts with review speed: invest in thoroughness. Each review round that finds a blocker prevents a more expensive implementation-phase discovery

name: output-format-matches-consumer-type
description: When the task consumer is a human decision-maker: produce markdown with tables and headers. When the consumer is a machine or data pipeline: produce structured JSON. Never default to JSON for human-facing deliverables
